text:
If you were hoping the smoke would stick around and ruin your Friday, tough luck, it's cleared out and today is genuinely gorgeous: sunny, light northwest wind, a high right around 81. Air quality's back to good, so no excuses to stay inside. The lake's calm too, waves 1 to 2 feet, water sitting at 72, about as inviting as it gets. Enjoy it, because the pattern flips fast.

Saturday is the one to plan around. The forecasters have actually upped the severe threat overnight, and both the timing and the smoke are messy, showers arriving as early as 9am, then a real chance of thunderstorms rolling in through the afternoon and evening, with wind on the lake building into the 15 to 25 knot range by night. The models are split on just how hot it gets first (anywhere from the mid 70s to mid 80s), but everyone agrees the second half of the day gets wet and gusty, with three quarters of an inch possible overnight. Get your outdoor plans done in the morning, and I'd have the boat back at the dock well before early afternoon.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-17T10:50:00+00:00): None, 64F feels 64F, humidity 56%, wind NW 7 mph
ALERTS: Air Quality Alert issued July 16 at 8:29AM EDT by NWS Burlington VT
NWS FORECAST:
  Today: Sunny, with a high near 81. Northwest wind around 8 mph.
  Tonight: Mostly cloudy, with a low around 60. Southeast wind 2 to 8 mph.
  Saturday: A chance of rain showers between 9am and 1pm, then patchy smoke and a slight chance of rain showers between 1pm and 2pm, then patchy smoke and a chance of showers and thunderstorms between 2pm and 3pm, then smoke and a chance of showers and thunderstorms between 3pm and 5pm, then patchy smoke and a chance of showers and thunderstorms. Mostly cloudy, with a high near 86. South wind 9 to 18 mph, with gusts as high as 29 mph. Chance of precipitation is 50%. New rainfall amounts between a tenth and quarter of an inch possible.
  Saturday Night: Showers and thunderstorms likely before 7pm, then a chance of showers and thunderstorms between 7pm and 8pm, then showers and thunderstorms between 8pm and 5am. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 61. Southwest wind 9 to 16 mph, with gusts as high as 28 mph. Chance of precipitation is 80%. New rainfall amounts between a half and three quarters of an inch possible.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-17T06:41:00+00:00):
  1. Thunderstorms and heavy rain expected Saturday afternoon and evening.
  2. Seasonable weather and intervals of wildfire smoke expected over the next few days.
  3. Frontal passages bring chances for showers on both Tuesday and Wednesday before high pressure dominates our weather on Thursday and Friday.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 240 AM EDT Friday... The severe threat for Saturday has increased.
LAKE (broad waters):
  Today: Northwest winds 10 knots or less. Sunny. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Tonight: Light and variable winds, becoming south 5 to 10 knots. Partly cloudy. Waves 1 foot or less.
  Saturday: South winds 10 to 15 knots, increasing to 15 to 25 knots. Mostly cloudy. A chance of showers in the morning, then a chance of showers and thunderstorms in the afternoon. Areas of smoke in the afternoon. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Saturday Night: South winds 15 to 25 knots, becoming northwest 15 to 20 knots. Showers with a chance of thunderstorms in the evening, then showers overnight. Waves 1 to 3 feet.
LAKE GAGE: water 72F, level 96.17 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-17: highs [GFS 79, Euro 75, ICON 77] spread 4F; precip chance [GFS 0%, Euro 0%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-18: highs [GFS 76, Euro 86, ICON 79] spread 10F; precip chance [GFS 85%, Euro 80%, ICON 88%]
  2026-07-19: highs [GFS 72, Euro 74, ICON 74] spread 2F; precip chance [GFS 73%, Euro 50%, ICON 75%]
AIR: AQI 28 Good (PM2.5) — On Friday, July 17th, expect generally good air quality across Vermont, as smoke from wildfires is expected to be blown southward of the state by a cold frontal passage.
SUN: rise 2026-07-17T05:24-04:00, set 2026-07-17T20:33-04:00, UV max 7.4
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high 80, low 60: Times of sun and clouds. Highs in the low 80s and lows in the low 60s.
  high 78, low 61: Afternoon showers and thunderstorms. Highs in the upper 70s and lows in the low 60s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: The smoke will begin to clear with Saturday evening’s storms and by Sunday things are looking calmer and clearer. A lingering shower is possible early Sunday, but then we’ll be drier through the afternoon with emerging sunshine. Temperatures will be in the 70s again.
